touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Casey Evans, VP of CS at PrimePathway. No answer, left voicemail introducing AskElephant's rep productivity platform.
Second cold call attempt to Casey Evans. No answer, no voicemail left this time.
Cold email sent to Casey Evans highlighting rep productivity gains and AI-powered insights. Referenced common challenges in fintech CS teams. No response received.

Brief qualification call with Casey Evans. New VP of CS (3 months in role) looking to scale team productivity without adding headcount. Currently evaluating conversation intelligence tools. Team of 9 AMs handling growing customer base. Expressed interest in demo.

questions_asked.question: What's driving your search for a conversation intelligence platform right now?
questions_asked.answer: We've doubled our customer base in 6 months but can't scale CS headcount proportionally. Need visibility into what's working in customer calls.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform differ from Gong? We've had a demo with them.
questions_asked.answer: Rep explained AskElephant's automation focus vs pure analytics, and fintech-specific compliance features.

next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation for next week
Send use case examples from fintech customers

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with fintech case studies and calendar link for technical eval. Casey confirmed interest and proposed time slots.

questions_asked.question: Can you share pricing ranges before the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Rep provided ballpark for 9 seats, mentioned volume discounts available.

touches.ai_summary: Quick call to confirm demo logistics and gather technical requirements. Casey mentioned Blake Rodriguez from procurement would need to be looped in eventually.

questions_asked.question: Who else should we include in the technical evaluation?
questions_asked.answer: Casey said to start with just her, will bring in Blake from procurement later if it looks good.

touches.ai_summary: 54-minute technical evaluation with Casey Evans. Walked through core platform features: call recording, AI summaries, automated follow-ups, and rep coaching workflows. Casey engaged positively on automation capabilities. Discussed integration with their Salesforce instance and compliance requirements for financial services. Gong comparison came up multiple times - Casey noted our automation features as differentiator but concerned about change management.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can reps adopt this without disrupting current workflows?
questions_asked.answer: Rep outlined 2-week rollout plan with training, emphasized low-touch integration.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Casey said ideally by end of quarter, but needs to get Blake and potentially finance involved.

questions_asked.question: What kind of discount can you offer for a small team like ours?
questions_asked.answer: Rep said they'd work up a proposal with competitive pricing for the 9 seats.

objections: Concerned about team adoption curve - new VP doesn't want to disrupt existing processes
next_steps: Loop in Blake Rodriguez for procurement review

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with formal proposal. 9 seats at $6,700 ACV (32% discount from list), unlimited automation tier. Highlighted ROI based on rep time savings. Casey acknowledged receipt.

Email thread with Casey and Blake Rodriguez. Blake raised questions about contract terms and budget approval process. Casey advocating internally but noted they need exec sign-off for software purchases over $5K.

questions_asked.question: Can we do a shorter initial term to test it out?
questions_asked.answer: Rep offered 12-month minimum, emphasized discount is contingent on annual commit.

touches.ai_summary: 38-minute meeting with Casey Evans and Blake Rodriguez (no exec sponsor present despite being flagged as requirement). Discussed pricing, implementation timeline, and contract terms. Blake focused heavily on cost justification and quarterly budget constraints. Casey reiterated value but less assertive than earlier calls. Negotiated payment terms and final discount.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on the annual commit? Our budget refresh is in Q3.
questions_asked.answer: Rep held firm on 12-month term but offered quarterly payment option.

questions_asked.question: Who from the exec team will be signing off on this?
questions_asked.answer: Blake said he has approval authority up to $10K, will route through CFO as FYI only.

questions_asked.question: How does this compare to the Gong proposal we have?
questions_asked.answer: Rep positioned automation features and pricing, asked if Gong offered similar discount.

objections: Blake concerned about budget timing - preferred to push to next quarter
next_steps: Send revised proposal with quarterly payments
Casey to circulate internally for final approval
Target signature by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with revised contract reflecting quarterly payment terms. Casey confirmed she's working on internal approvals but mentioned 'a few more hoops to jump through.'

Check-in email on contract status. Casey responded that they're in final review and expects to have decision by early next week. No urgency or strong close signals. Blake hasn't responded to rep's direct outreach.
